rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use visible identifiers and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use AGREE logical operators for branching logic and implications
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for declarations
anti_patterns: Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use opaque Boolean data range comparisons unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
